files.SKILL.md:
Before you write a line, check what prior sessions recorded — both notes and any test outcome files available. A failing test name tells you the exact class of mistake to avoid. Go straight to the fix instead of re-investigating from scratch.

Every task has a boundary. Find it before implementing. The natural implementation handles the middle — it breaks at the exact limit. Ask: what happens at precisely the threshold, not one below and not one above? Make sure your code answers that correctly.

Touch only what the task requires. Each file changed beyond the minimum is a file that can break something that was passing. If you notice something adjacent that needs fixing, write it down. Do not fix it now.

After a change that matters, verify it landed. Look at the result directly. A successful response is not the same as a correct outcome.

Before you exit, write one structural observation to the persistent notes — the class of mistake, what the wrong path looks like, what the correct path looks like. Not the specific names from this task. Those rotate. The class does not.

When the task is to document a bug: name the class, cite the specific failing tests by exact name, state the root cause, show the fix. A document a stranger could use to avoid the same mistake is complete. One that describes symptoms without those details is not.
